name: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum)
summary: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) is a yellow-flowered herb with well-studied antidepressant effects for mild-to-moderate depression. Its active constituents (hypericin, hyperforin) inhibit reuptake of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy comparable to low-dose SSRIs for mild depression, with fewer side effects in that population.
description: St. John's wort centers on the flowering tops.
safety: Clinically significant drug interactions via CYP3A4 and CYP2C9 induction. Can substantially reduce blood levels of oral contraceptives, warfarin, cyclosporine, antiretrovirals, and many other medications. Do not combine with SSRIs or other serotonergic drugs due to serotonin syndrome risk. Photosensitivity reported at high doses. Consult a clinician before use if taking any prescription medications.
dosage: Standardized extract (0.3% hypericin or 2-5% hyperforin): ~900 mg/day in divided doses; formulation-dependent
typical_dosage: Standardized extract (0.3% hypericin or 2-5% hyperforin): ~900 mg/day in divided doses; formulation-dependent
affiliate_query: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) capsule, tea, or standardized extract supplement
buying_criteria: active marker or extract ratio disclosed

sources.title: St. John's wort (Hypericum perforatum) and depression: what happens to the neurotransmitter systems?
